characters.name: Beverly Ann Stickle
characters.description: Beverly Ann StickelBirthdate1926BirthplaceAppleton, WisconsinFamilyMr, Stickle (father, deceased), Mrs. Stickle (deceased),Edna Garrett(sister), Raymond Garrett, Alex Garrett (nephews), Bruce Gaines (former brother-in-law, possibly deceased)Appearancethin and regalPersonalityoptimistic, quirkyRelationshipsunknownFateunrevealed Beverly Ann Sticklewas a fictional character on the TV series, "The Facts of Life." She was played by film and TV actressCloris Leachmanin forty-eight episodes of the series between 1986 to 1988. Beverly Ann Stickle was the divorced sister ofEdna Garrett. Reuniting with Edna after her marriage in 1986, she moved into Edna'sPeekskillafter Edna moved to Africa to serve in the Peace Corps with her new husband. In doing so, she became close friends withBlair Warner,Natalie Green,Jo PolniaczekandTootie Ramsey, Edna's partners in theOver Our Heads Store. Mrs. Garrett also wanted someone to keep an eye on them as she had, and she became the property owner of Edna's house and the store. She also became the foster mother toAndy Moffett, to prevent him from being shuffled from foster home to foster home.

characters.name: Blair Warner
characters.description: Blair WarnerBirthdate1965BirthplaceNew York CityFamilyDavid Warner(father),Monica Warner(mother),Meg(step-sister),Bailey Warner(half-sister),Geri Tyler(cousin), Carlton Blair (grandfather, deceased)Appearanceattractive, blonde, shapelyPersonalityexuberant, vivacious, somewhat self-centeredRelationshipsSteve,Cliff Whitfeld, Tad WarnerFatehotel heiress and attorneyBlair Warnerwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." She was played by actressLisa Whelchelthroughout the series and onThe Facts of Life Reunionand in several movies. Blair's hometown was New York City, although she mentioned having relatives in Texas. In the episode "Magnificent Obsession", Tootie said that Blair was born two months prematurely and hasn't been early for anything since. She was an only child until her half-sister, Bailey, was born when Blair was 20-years-old. Blair Warner was the daughter ofDavidandMonica Warner. David Warner owned Warner Textile Mills, making him a multi-millionaire and Blair an heiress to his fortune. She had a cousin namedGeri Tyler. Blair described having many stepsiblings over the years due to her parents' frequent new marriages. Eventually, her mother remarried for the fourth time and gave birth to Bailey. Initially, Blair was excited about her mother's pregnancy, while her mother wasn't sure about continuing it, which led to conflict between them. Blair even offered to raise Bailey if Blair's mother wasn't willing to parent her. Ultimately, Blair's mother decides she does want to be a mom again and Bailey was born on Christmas Day with Blair serving as her mother's labor coach.

characters.name: Cindy Webster
characters.description: Cindy WebsterBirthdate1965BirthplaceUnrevealedFamilyUnrevealedAppearancepetite, blonde,Personalityfunny, tom boy, athleticRelationshipsunknownFatebecame a model under the name "Cindy Baker" Cindy Websterwas a fictional character on the TV series, "The Facts of Life." She was played by child actressJulie Anne Haddockin seventeen episodes of the series and one episode of "Diff'rent Strokes." Cindy Webster was a student atEastland Academywhere she was good friends with the studentsSue Ann Weaver,Molly Parker,Natalie GreenandTootie Ramsey. Among her dorm sisters wasBlair WarnerandNancy Olson. Although she was on friendly terms with Blair, they did have some rivalry.In school, Cindy was very much a tomboy and quite athletic, sometimes questioning her femininity and gender identity after Blair made some disparaging remarks about her after she elected to run for Harvest Queen at school. She actually became runner-up, winning the title "Corn Maiden of the Harvest." She was also very sports-minded, participating in baseball, tennis, hockey, and track.In1986, Cindy along withSue Ann WeaverandNancy Olsonreturned toPeekskillto visit Blair, Tootie and Natalie atOver Our Heads, where it was revealed that she now goes under the name Cindy Baker as a fashion model.

characters.name: Cliff Whitfield
characters.description: Cliff Whitfieldis a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." He was played by actorWoody Brownthrough five episodes of the series, Cliff Whitfield is a native ofPeekskill, New York, and a one-time love interest ofBlair Warner. Dating through 1984, 1985 and 1986, he was studying to be a doctor, working his way as an exotic dancer at the Wedgewood, a fact he kept from Blair. When she andJo Polniaczekwent to the Wedgewood to celebrateEdna Garrett's birthday, Blair recognized him, and she raced out embarrassed. The incident strained their relationship, but they gradually worked their way through it.

characters.name: Cynthia
characters.description: Cynthiais a fictional character from "The Facts Of Life". She was played by actressDenise Halmain one episode. Cynthia (last name unrevealed) was a student atEastland Academy. She ran for Student Council President againstBlairand won. However, Cynthia's life wasn't what the other girls andMrs. Garrettthought it was: she barely saw her diplomat father, had to move around a lot, her parents were getting divorced, she put pressure on herself to succeed, and Blair's disgruntlement in losing drove her to commit suicide.

characters.description: Eddie Brennanis a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." He was played by actorClark Brandonin four episodes of the series.

characters.name: Edna Garrett
characters.description: Edna GarrettBirthdate1926 (estimated)BirthplaceAppleton, WisconsinFamilyRaymond Garrett,Alex Garrett(sons),Beverly Ann Stickle(sister),Bruce Gaines(third husband, deceased)Appearanceshort, older woman, once large frame but now thinPersonalityfriendly, maternal, animatedRelationshipsUnrevealedFateUnrevealed Edna Garrettwas a fictional character on "Different Strokes" and "The Facts of Life." She was played by stage actressCharlotte Raefrom 1978 to 1986 and in the TV movie,The Facts of Life Reunion Edna Ann Garrett Gaines was the youngest child in a large family born and raised on a farm near Appleton, Wisconsin. Her exact age is unknown, but it was often hinted or implied that she was somewhere in her fifties. For several years, she was the housekeeper to the Drummond family in New York City and the maternal mother figure of Philip Drummond's daughter, Kimberly Drummond, and later, his adopted sons, Willis and Arnold Jackson after their mother, the previous housekeeper died. However, in 1979, she accepted a job as den mother for the girls at Kimberly's school,Eastland Academy, inPeekskill, New York.Over the years, Mrs. Garrett's role at Eastland changed. At first, she was merely a live-in supervisor for the girls of Eastland, and later, she became their in-house dietician who managed the school's cafeteria. She had been married twice, divorced in her first marriage) and widowed in her second marriage. Her first husband eventually returned to woo her and reconcile, but he was unsuccessful because Edna felt that his gambling problem would always be an obstacle. She had two sons, Alex, a singer-songwriter/carpenter, and Raymond, an accountant. Raymond helped her raise funds and secure commercial space so she could open her own gourmet food shop,Edna's Edibles, after she became disenchanted with her life at Eastland in the fall of of 1983. She also became a mentor to the girls at Eastland, mostly toBlair Warner,Jo Polniaczek,Natalie GreenandTootie Ramsey, functioning in loco parentis. At times, the girls would take her for granted and forget that Edna herself had problems. Many times Edna would lash out at the girls when they got careless with her, but they always came back stronger than before. They lived with her at Eastland over the school kitchen in the administration building and worked with her in the kitchen to pay off various restitution-related expenses and soon moved into an apartment above the shop and continued to work for her in the shop.In 1985, Edna's Edibles was extensively damaged by fire, and it was rebuilt as a gift shop calledOver Our Heads. Since the insurance on Edna's Edibles had lapsed by the time of the fire, the girls contributed their insurance claim checks to help rebuild it, effectively making Mrs. Garrett and the girls equal partners in the business.  She once actually fired them when their irresponsible behavior ended up costing her $500 in fines after a dismal health inspection, but she hired them back the next day when

characters.name: Emily Mahoney
characters.description: Emily MahoneyBirthdate1942BirthplaceUnrevealedFamilyUnrevealedAppearancebrunettePersonalityeducated, meticulousRelationshipsunknownFateunrevealed Emily Mahoneywas a fictional character on the TV series, "The Facts of Life." She was played by film and stage actressJenny O'Harain four episodes in the series. Emily Mahoney was a history and science teacher atEastland Academyin1979. Known as "Miss Emily" to her students, she also assisted headmasterSteven Bradleyin his administration at the school, but she was often being challenged or undermined by his choices to bend or circumvent the school rules to favor the students. BeforeEdna Garrett, she tried to serve as a counselor and mentor for the girls on campus, a role that Mrs. Garrett later took over.

characters.name: George Burnett
characters.description: George BurnettBirthdate1961BirthplaceunrevealedFamilyNone knownAppearancetall, attractive, brunettePersonalityfriendly, wittyRelationshipscoming upFateunrevealedGeorge Burnettwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." He was played by actorGeorge Clooneyof "Ocean's Eleven" fame through seventeen episodes of the series. George Burnett is a local carpenter and contractor who worked for his father at the local hardware store inPeekskill, New York. Not much is known about his life, but after the local delicacy shopEdna's Ediblesburned down in 1985, he was hired as a handyman to help rebuild the store intoOver Our Heads. In doing so, he became close friends of the girls living there, particularlyBlair Warner,Natalie GreenandJo Polniaczek, who he asked to pretend to be his girlfriend to make an old flame jealous. However, he was eventually fired when his work began lagging, but when faced with more dishonest contractors,Edna Garrettsoon hired him back. Two years later, George metCinnamon, a pop star who was friends of the girls. Stressed by the pressures of her fame, she confessed she wanted to walk away from it, but George convinced her to stay with it, even going as far to leave Peekskill and help her as a roadie in her concert.

characters.name: Geri Tyler
characters.description: Geri TylerBirthdateUnrevealedBirthplaceUnrevealedFamilyMr. Tyler (father),David Warner(uncle),Blair Warner,Megan Warner,Bailey Warner(cousins),AppearancebrunettePersonalityfriendly, funny, has cerebral palsyRelationshipsunrevealedFateunrevealedGeri Tylerwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." She was played by comedian and actressGeri Jewellthrough twelve episodes in the series. Geri Jewell is a successful comedian and entertainer related the wealthy Warner Family of New York. Among her family areBlair WarnerandMonica Warnerbut it is unrevealed how she is related to them. Given her name is Tyler, it is likely they are relatives on her mother's side of the family.

characters.name: Jaime Bonner
characters.description: Jaime Bonneris a fictional character in "The Facts of Life Reunion." She was played by actressMallory Margel. Jaime Bonner is the daughter of musical promoterRick BonnerandJo Polniaczek. Not much is known about her past, but her lifestyle seems eerily similar to Jo's years as a teenage rebel. LikeTisha Ramsey, the daughter ofTootie Ramsey, she counts her mother's best friends as mentors, includingBlair Warner,Natalie Green, andTootie Ramsey. She also knewEdna Garrett,who got to know her growing up through computer videos. Jamie even refers to Blair as "Aunt Blair," implying a close connection between the families.

characters.name: Jo Polniaczek
characters.description: Jo PolniaczekBirthdate1965BirthplaceNew York CityFamilyRose Polniaczek(mother)Charlie Polniaczek(father),Rick Bonner(husband)Jaime Bonner(daughter)Appearanceattractive, thin, athleticPersonalitytough, sarcastic, cynicalRelationshipsEddie BrennanFateBecame a police detectiveJoanna "Jo" Polniaczekwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." She appeared at the start ofSeason Twoand was played by actressNancy McKeonthrough the rest of the series and two movies. Joanna "Jo" Marie Polniaczek was Polish-American through her dad's side and Sicilian-American through her mom's side. She was a native of the Bronx in New York City. Her father, Charlie, served time in jail, and her mother, Rose, raised her primarily as a single parent. Jo was an only child, but had several cousins, including her cousin Terry, whom she visited in season two.  In "The Interview Show," she told a story about how, as a child, she once fell off of a roof and landed on a mattress that a neighbor was throwing out. She often described difficult childhood situations and a tough upbringing. She began attendingEastland Academyin her sophomore year of high school. She used to dateEddie Brennan, but after they got in trouble for unrevealed activity, and after Jo joined a street gang known as "The Young Diablos", her mother sent her to Eastland to get her out of the city. Jo said that her mother thought she and Eddie were getting too serious for her age. Arriving atEastland Academyon her motorcycle, she at first clashed with her assigned roommate,Blair Warner, considering her stuck-up and over-privileged. Likewise, Blair found Jo classless and masculine in her attire. However, her relationship with Blair became close as time went on (Jo would even refer to Blair as her best friend by the last season), although the two still argued, traded wisecracks, and made fun of each other from time to time.

characters.name: Jo Polniaczek
characters.description: Jo PolniaczekBirthdate1965BirthplaceNew York CityFamilyRose Polniaczek(mother)Charlie Polniaczek(father),Rick Bonner(husband)Jaime Bonner(daughter)Appearanceattractive, thin, athleticPersonalitytough, sarcastic, cynicalRelationshipsEddie BrennanFateBecame a police detectiveJoanna "Jo" Polniaczekwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." Her father, Charlie, served time in jail, and her mother, Rose, raised her primarily as a single parent. Jo was an only child, but had several cousins, including her cousin Terry, whom she visited in season two.  In "The Interview Show," she told a story about how, as a child, she once fell off of a roof and landed on a mattress that a neighbor was throwing out. She often described difficult childhood situations and a tough upbringing. She began attendingEastland Academyin her sophomore year of high school. She used to dateEddie Brennan, but after they got in trouble for unrevealed activity, and after Jo joined a street gang known as "The Young Diablos", her mother sent her to Eastland to get her out of the city. Jo said that her mother thought she and Eddie were getting too serious for her age. Arriving atEastland Academyon her motorcycle, she at first clashed with her assigned roommate,Blair Warner, considering her stuck-up and over-privileged. Likewise, Blair found Jo classless and masculine in her attire. However, her relationship with Blair became close as time went on (Jo would even refer to Blair as her best friend by the last season), although the two still argued, traded wisecracks, and made fun of each other from time to time.

characters.name: Kimberly Drummond
characters.description: Kimberly DrummondBirthdate1964BirthplaceNew York CityFamilyPhilip Drummond(father), Mrs. Drummond (mother, deceased), Margaret McKinney-Drummond (step-mother),Arnold Jackson,Willis Jackson(foster brothers), Sam McKinney (step-brother)Appearanceblonde, attractivePersonalitycharming, vivaciousRelationshipsunrevealedFateunknownKimberly Drummondwas a fictional character on "Diff'rent Strokes" and "The Facts of Life." She was played by child actressDana Plato, appearing in 140 episodes of "Diff'rent Strokes," one episode of "The Facts Of Life" and one episode of "Hello, Larry."

characters.name: Local Residents
characters.description: This page lists all of the main, recurring and supporting characters on "The Facts of Life."

characters.name: Margo
characters.description: Margois a fictional character from "The Facts Of Life." She was played by actressAmy Gibsonin one episode. Margo (last name unrevealed) was a student atEastland Academyin 1980. Not much is known about her, but she seems to be friends with classmateMolly Parkerand might be the same age as her. After the start of the 1980 semester, she and Molly were having lunch in the school cafeteria, and Margo started mockingJo Polniaczekcleaning the tables to pay off damages to the school van. Even going as far as telling her to clean her table, she raked the crumbs on the table toward Jo and called her "a good worker" despite Molly telling her to stop haranguing Jo, who then brushed the table on to her. Standing her ground, Jo didn't lose her temper until Margo mentioned her mother. Before Jo could respond,Blair Warnerstepped in to defend her by smacking Marge in the face with partially eaten pie.

characters.name: Molly Parker
characters.description: Molly ParkerBirthdate1968BirthplaceUnrevealedFamilyMr. Parker (father), Mrs. Parker (mother)Appearancepetite, Dutch girl haircutPersonalityrational, leadership principlesRelationshipscoming upFatebecame an actressMolly Parkerwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." She was played by film actressMolly Ringwaldof John Hughes fame, appearing in fourteen episodes and two episodes of "Diff'rent Strokes." Molly Parker is a student at theEastland Academy for Girls, where she is friends withKimberly Drummond,Cindy Webster,Tootie RamseyandNatalie Green. Other girls in her dormitory includeBlair Warner,Nancy OlsonandSue Ann Weaver. Their house mother wasEdna Garrett, who later became the school dietician. Her favorite activity is photography, often taking pictures for yearbooks and the school newspaper. She also likes playing the acoustic guitar and is known as a fast talker. It is not known if she has any siblings, but it is known her parents are divorced, occurring while everyone was getting ready to go home for spring break. As a result, she was in a bad mood, and refused to go home, but her friends in the dorm rallied around her. She is very close to her friends, having stood up forSue Ann Weaverwhen she was being teased by Blair for dressing like a boy.

characters.name: Monica Warner
characters.description: Monica WarnerBirthdateUnrevealedBirthplacepossibly New York CityFamilyCarlton Blair (father, deceased),David Warner(former husband),Blair Warner,Megan Warner,Bailey Warner(daughters),Geri Tyler(niece),Appearanceattractive, brunettePersonalityexuberant, vivacious, worldlyRelationshipscoming upFateunrevealedMonica Warnerwas a fictional character on "The Facts of Life." She was played by actressesPam HuntingdonandMarj Dusaythrough nine episodes in the series. Monica Warner is a wealthy socialite and traveler and the mother ofBlair WarnerandBailey Warner. Not much is known about her past, but her maiden name might be Blair. She was once married to industrialistDavid Warner, but the marriage didn't last and she afterward started dating several beaus and going through several other marriages. She has doted heavily on Blair, and Blair often mentions her frequently to her friends, even as far as going to her mother's associates for favors, such as asking a judge to track downNatalie Green's biological mother. Monica and Blair are very close, and many of her personality traits are also in Blair. Blair was once concerned over her mother's flirting with other men, but she later came to terms with it. However, Monica has had to make choices Blair didn't like, such as agreeing withEdna Garrettto let Blair and her friends work in the school kitchen to pay off the damages to the school van.
